message.content:
You are implementing Task 3 of a GitHub Phase 2 Data Ingestion feature in a NestJS/TypeScript backend.

## Working directory
`/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend`

## Context
Read these files first to understand exact patterns to follow:
- `src/modules/github/db/github-repos.model.ts` — the model pattern to follow exactly
- `src/modules/github/types/github-contributor.interface.ts`
- `src/modules/github/types/github-commit.interface.ts`
- `src/modules/github/types/github-pull-request.interface.ts`
- `src/modules/github/types/github-review.interface.ts`
- `src/modules/github/types/github-review-comment.interface.ts`

## Code rules (CRITICAL)
- No comments in code
- No relative imports — use `@modules/`, `@constants/` aliases only
- All functions must have explicit return types
- Single object destructuring parameter for all functions
- No `console.log` — use NestJS Logger

## Files to create

### 1. `src/modules/github/db/github-contributor.model.ts`
Methods:
- `upsert({ data: IGithubContributorUpsert }): Promise<void>` — insert with onConflict(['installation_id', 'github_user_id']).merge(['github_login', 'github_name', 'github_email', 'updated_at'])
- `findByInstallationAndLogin({ installationId, githubLogin }): Promise<IGithubContributorBase | null>`

### 2. `src/modules/github/db/github-commit.model.ts`
Methods:
- `upsertMany({ commits: IGithubCommitUpsert[] }): Promise<void>` — bulk insert, guard on empty array, onConflict(['repo_id', 'sha']).merge(['message', 'author_login', 'author_name', 'author_email', 'additions', 'deletions', 'updated_at'])
- `findByRepoId({ repoId }): Promise<IGithubCommitBase[]>` — orderBy committed_at desc

### 3. `src/modules/github/db/github-pull-request.model.ts`
Methods:
- `upsert({ data: IGithubPullRequestUpsert }): Promise<IGithubPullRequestBase>` — insert with onConflict(['repo_id', 'pr_number']).merge([...all updatable fields...]).returning('*'), return first row
- `findByRepoAndNumber({ repoId, prNumber }): Promise<IGithubPullRequestBase | null>`

### 4. `src/modules/github/db/github-review.model.ts`
Methods:
- `upsert({ data: IGithubReviewUpsert }): Promise<void>` — onConflict(['pull_request_id', 'github_review_id']).merge(['state', 'reviewer_login', 'submitted_at', 'updated_at'])
- `findByPullRequestId({ pullRequestId }): Promise<IGithubReviewBase[]>` — orderBy submitted_at asc

### 5. `src/modules/github/db/github-review-comment.model.ts`
Methods:
- `upsert({ data: IGithubReviewCommentUpsert }): Promise<void>` — onConflict(['pull_request_id', 'github_comment_id']).merge(['body', 'path', 'line', 'author_login', 'commented_at', 'updated_at'])
- `findByPullRequestId({ pullRequestId }): Promise<IGithubReviewCommentBase[]>` — orderBy commented_at asc

### 6. ALSO add this method to EXISTING `src/modules/github/db/github-repos.model.ts`:
```typescript
async findByGithubRepoId({ githubRepoId }: { githubRepoId: number }): Promise<IGithubRepoBase | null> {
    try {
        const result = await this.knexRead<IGithubRepoBase>(this.tableName)
            .where({ github_repo_id: githubRepoId })
            .first();
        return result ?? null;
    } catch (error) {
        this.logger.error(`Failed to find repo by github_repo_id ${githubRepoId}: ${(error as Error).message}`);
        throw error;
    }
}
```

## After implementing
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend && npm run type-check
```
Fix any errors. Then:
```bash
git add src/modules/github/db/
git commit -m "feat: add db models for github data ingestion"
```

Report: DONE
